_readme: SIP Tool v4.0 — Configuration File. Edit values below to customize behavior.
_data_source: All data fetched from OpenAlex API (openalex.org) — free, open academic database
_reference_warning: References are AUTO-GENERATED and may be incomplete. Always verify before academic use.
_per_page_note: Papers per page. Max allowed by OpenAlex: 200. Recommended: 25-50
_max_pages_note: Pages to fetch. Total papers = per_page × max_pages. Example: 25 × 4 = 100
_polite_email_note: Add your email for higher API rate limits. Example: 'you@email.com'. OpenAlex polite pool gives priority to identified tools.
_text_mode_note: How to split abstract text. Options: 'paragraph' (by newline), 'sentence' (by . ! ?), 'full' (whole abstract). 'paragraph' is best for most cases.
_match_mode_note: How keywords must match. Options: 'all' (every keyword required - strict), 'any' (one keyword enough - loose), 'phrase' (exact phrase match)
_min_word_count_note: Text blocks shorter than this are ignored. Increase to remove short/useless sentences.
_min_abstract_words_note: Abstracts shorter than this are skipped entirely.
_max_blocks_note: Maximum text blocks extracted per paper. Increase to get more content per paper.
_year_note: Only include papers published in this year range.
_citation_note: Only include papers cited at least this many times. 0 = include all. Example: 100 = highly cited only.
_oa_note: Set to true to only get papers with free full text available online.
_language_note: Filter by language code. 'en' = English only. Leave empty for all languages.
_author_note: Filter by author name (partial match). Example: 'Smith' matches any paper by an author named Smith.
_institution_note: Filter by institution. Example: 'Harvard' matches papers from Harvard researchers.
_concepts_note: Filter by topic/concept tags. Example: ['Machine Learning', 'Neural Networks']
_sort_note: How OpenAlex sorts results. Options: 'cited_by_count:desc' (most cited first, RECOMMENDED), 'publication_date:desc' (newest first), 'cited_by_count:asc' (least cited first)
_output_note: Main output file — used by index.html to display results in browser.
_export_note: Formats to generate. Available: 'json', 'csv', 'bibtex', 'ris', 'markdown', 'txt'. Add or remove as needed.
_oa_url_note: Include direct link to free full text when available.
_abstract_note: Include the full reconstructed abstract in JSON output.
_ref_style_note: Reference format. Currently: 'apa7' (APA 7th edition). WARNING: Auto-generated, may be incomplete.
_ref_disclaimer: References generated by this tool are AUTOMATIC approximations. Known issues: missing volume/issue/pages, approximate author formatting, inconsistent journal names. ALWAYS verify before academic submission. Use CrossRef (crossref.org) to get complete metadata.
_cache_note: Cache stores API responses locally. Repeated searches are instant and don't use API quota.
_cache_dir_note: Folder for cache files. Starting with '.' makes it hidden on Linux/Mac.
_cache_ttl_note: How long to keep cached data before refreshing (hours). 24 = 1 day.
_dedup_note: Remove duplicate papers from results. Duplicates can occur across multiple pages.
_crossref_note: Try CrossRef API for missing volume/issue/pages. Makes references more complete but slower. Even CrossRef can be incomplete.
_history_note: Save a log of all searches. View with: python api.py --history
_timeout_note: Seconds to wait for API response before giving up.
_retry_note: How many times to retry a failed request.
_retry_delay_note: Seconds to wait between retry attempts.
_verbose_note: Show detailed debug info in terminal. Set to true for troubleshooting.
_authors_note: Max authors shown in references. Authors beyond this become 'et al.'
